code: # A throwaway package that (deliberately) uses T/F instead of TRUE/FALSE bad_pkg <- example_diagnose_scenario("code_examples/tf_usage_bad.R") #> === Example file: tf_usage_bad.R === #> # Example file showing T/F usage issues #>  #> #' Process Data Function #> #' @param data A data frame #> #' @return Logical indicating success #> process_data <- function(data) { #>   if (is.null(data)) { #>     return(F)  # Issue: should be FALSE #>   } #>    #>   has_complete_cases <- T  # Issue: should be TRUE #>    #>   if (has_complete_cases) { #>     cleaned_data <- data[complete.cases(data), ] #>     return(T)  # Issue: should be TRUE #>   } #>    #>   return(F)  # Issue: should be FALSE #> } #>  #> # Another function with T/F issues #> validate_input <- function(x, strict = T) {  # Issue: should be TRUE #>   if (length(x) == 0) return(F)  # Issue: should be FALSE #>    #>   valid <- all(is.numeric(x)) #>   return(valid && strict == T)  # Issue: should be TRUE #> } #>  #> === End of example === #>  #> Temporary package created at: /tmp/RtmpNd5aAM/checktor_example_20260626_003507_7484  #> Example file copied to: /tmp/RtmpNd5aAM/checktor_example_20260626_003507_7484/R/tf_usage_bad.R  checkup(bad_pkg) #> [1] FALSE

title: Writing Your Own checktor Checks
text: Parses every R/*.R file package returns named list list(file, xml, error). parse failure becomes error slot instead crashing run. xml slot xml2 document produced xmlparsedata::xml_parse_data(). Every parse-tree token XML element line1, col1, line2, col2 attributes. Runs XPath query every parsed file returns "basename:line" strings, suitable assignment check result’s $issues. optional label appears parens hit. common pattern — “flag call function X” — canned helper: checktor’s equivalent lintr::undesirable_function_linter(). Returns XPath predicate restricts hits nodes whose innermost enclosing function-body doesn’t also contain call funs. option_changes enforces options() guarded sibling .exit() function — “innermost” part makes correct nested functions .exit outer function wouldn’t cover inner one. Walking .Rd files structurally via tools::parse_Rd():
code: parsed <- read_r_xml(".") str(parsed[[1]]) #> List of 3 #>  $ file : chr "R/foo.R" #>  $ xml  : xml_document #>  $ error: NULL hits <- xpath_lints(parsed,                     "//SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL[text() = 'set.seed']") #> "foo.R:42" "bar.R:17" issues <- undesirable_function_check(parsed,                                      c("install.packages", "browser")) predicate <- not_under_fn_with_call_xpath(c("on.exit", "local_options")) xpath <- paste0(   "//SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL[text() = 'options']",   "[", predicate, "]" ) rd <- tools::parse_Rd("man/my_fn.Rd") ex <- extract_rd_section(rd, "\\examples") collect_rd_text(ex, skip = "\\dontrun")

title: Writing Your Own checktor Checks
text: Suppose want check flags Sys.setenv() call whose enclosing function doesn’t also call .exit(Sys.unsetenv(...)) withr::local_envvar(). shape diagnose_option_changes ships checktor diagnose_sys_setenv_no_reset. ’s implementation: Twenty lines. interesting part XPath predicate — everything else bookkeeping shared every check.
code: diagnose_sys_setenv_no_reset <- function(path, verbose = TRUE,                                          parsed = NULL) {   if (is.null(parsed)) parsed <- read_r_xml(path)   if (length(parsed) == 0L) {     return(checktor_check_result(TRUE, character(0),                                  "Sys.setenv reset check"))   }   xpath <- paste0(     "//SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL[text() = 'Sys.setenv'][",     "  ", not_under_fn_with_call_xpath(c(         "on.exit",         "Sys.unsetenv",         "local_envvar", "with_envvar"       )),     "]"   )   issues <- xpath_lints(parsed, xpath)   passed <- length(issues) == 0L   checktor_check_result(passed, issues, "Sys.setenv reset check") }

title: Writing Your Own checktor Checks
text: call fn(, b = 1) parses : anchor SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL: call expr parent::expr/parent::expr first positional arg parent::expr/following-sibling::expr[1] named-arg name parent::expr/parent::expr/SYMBOL_SUB common bug treating parent::expr call expr — ’s actually function-name wrapper, one child (SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL ).
code: <expr>                              <!-- call expr -->   <expr>                            <!-- function-name expr -->     <SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL>fn</SYMBOL_FUNCTION_CALL>   </expr>   <OP-LEFT-PAREN>(   <expr><SYMBOL>a</SYMBOL></expr>   <!-- first positional arg -->   <OP-COMMA>,   <SYMBOL_SUB>b</SYMBOL_SUB>        <!-- named-arg name -->   <EQ_SUB>=</EQ_SUB>   <expr><NUM_CONST>1</NUM_CONST></expr>  <!-- named-arg value -->   <OP-RIGHT-PAREN>) </expr>

title: Diagnose Package for CRAN Submission Issues — checktor
text: checktor_results object (list) containing: code_issues: Results code diagnostics description_issues: Results DESCRIPTION file diagnostics documentation_issues: Results documentation diagnostics general_issues: Results general package diagnostics policy_issues: Results CRAN policy violation diagnostics metadata: List package path, diagnosis time, total issue count, total failed-check count, checktor version diagnostic category contains passed element showing individual checks passed/failed, plus detailed results check.

title: Diagnose Package for CRAN Submission Issues — checktor
text: function runs five categories diagnostics: Code, DESCRIPTION, Documentation, General, Policy. See diagnose_code_issues(), diagnose_description_issues(), diagnose_documentation_issues(), diagnose_general_issues(), diagnose_policy_violations() specific checks within category. metadata$total_issues figure counts total number distinct issues found across checks (e.g., 80 lines using T/F count 80, 1). metadata$failed_checks figure counts many individual checks reported issue .

Options: "minimal" (basic fields ), "bad" (known issues), "good" (properly formatted). Default: "minimal". cleanup Logical. Whether register cleanup temporary directory exit. Default: FALSE (user manages cleanup).

Issue: should be FALSE #>   } #>    #>   has_complete_cases <- T  # Issue: should be TRUE #>    #>   if (has_complete_cases) { #>     cleaned_data <- data[complete.cases(data), ] #>     return(T)  # Issue: should be TRUE #>   } #>    #>   return(F)  # Issue: should be TRUE #> } #>  #> === End of example === #>  #> Temporary package created at: /tmp/Rtmpo4vEI8/checktor_example_20260626_003504_4331  #> Example file copied to: /tmp/Rtmpo4vEI8/checktor_example_20260626_003504_4331/R/tf_usage_bad.R  #>  result <- diagnose_tf_usage(pkg_path, verbose = TRUE) #> ✖ Found `T`/`F` usage (should use `TRUE`/`FALSE`) #> • tf_usage_bad.R:8 #> • tf_usage_bad.R:11 #> • tf_usage_bad.R:15 #> • tf_usage_bad.R:18 #> • tf_usage_bad.R:22 #> ... and 2 more cat("Issues found:", length(result$issues)) #> Issues found: 7  # title: checktor 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Adds [checktor()] top-level orchestrator, running five categories diagnostics (code, DESCRIPTION, documentation, general, CRAN policy) R package directory. Adds [checkup()] boolean wrapper CI use, [prescribe()] treatment recommendations, [health_report()] Markdown / HTML / text reports. code-side diagnostics run XPath queries parsed AST via xmlparsedata + xml2. Documentation-side checks walk .Rd files via tools::parse_Rd(). DESCRIPTION parsed base::read.dcf().
